title: Experience with cougars
description: Sisks homesteaded near Princeton in late eighties; experiences with cougars.

title: Work of mothers
description: Home grown food. Mothers worked from daylight to dark. Washing and ironing.

title: Currant bush mishap
description: Mrs. Wurman knocks down a currant bush by mistake.

title: The Lynds sell site to Potlatch Lumber Company
description: The Lynds sell part of site of Potlatch to the Potlatch Lumber Company.

title: Log drives on Palouse River
description: Log drives on the Palouse River.

title: School and games
description: One room school. Style of clothes. Playing Ante Over, Dare Base, Black Man, Steal Sticks, Pussy Wants a Corner.

title: Community relations
description: Community paper was read at school get-togethers, telling what people were doing. The whole family went to dances and community get-togethers. An all night affair, with a big feed in the morning.

title: Courting and fear of not marrying
description: Courting with the parents, dressed well and at home. Fear of being an old maid. How Mary met Reuben Lynd.

title: Work of women
description: Girls' work in the harvest and around the farm. Fanning mills. Learning to cook young.

title: Literary and intellectual life
description: Literary and debate. Acting in a play. Lanterns. What people talked about.

title: Women's health and child bearing issues
description: Doctoring was done by neighbors at home. Having babies at home. Midwives. Ten days of enforced rest in bed after delivery. Home cures, Home cures: mentholatum, Epsom salts. Children's sicknesses; scarlet fever.

title: Childraising and family life
description: Raising the children. Strictness of fathers. Families lived together - mother stayed at home, and never thought of working out. There was little reason to go to town; Saturday shopping, movies, and circus. People no longer know their neighbors.

title: Timber between Palouse and Potlatch
description: Timber between Palouse and Potlatch.

title: Dancing, parties, and married life
description: Acquaintance with the Daileys. Learning to two-step. Shivarees were noise-making occasions. Avoiding the shivaree. Starting marriage in a chicken house. Starting marriage with little resources.

title: Lynds and Wurman family histories
description: The Lynds buy railroad land in Montana in 1919, and come back four years later. The Wurmans farmed in Canada in 1918, had four crop failures and traded for Okanogan land; Mamie stayed there for many years, reluctantly.

title: Rubbernecks on telephone line; hauling water; school life
description: Rubbernecks on the telephone line. A son pulls a joke on a woman on the phone. Isolation of the Okanogan. Living without modern conveniences - hauling water. Remoteness of school near Princeton.

title: Household work with food and animals
description: Butchering and preservation of meat. Mamie Munden's mother forces her husband to build a smokehouse. Making apple butter; making soap. Keeping pigs. Getting through hard times.

title: Raising kids
description: Kids took care of each other, so it was easy to raise a family. Acceptance of new babies in the family.

title: Rarity of divorce and working couples
description: Divorce extremely rare in early days. Couples who work don't have time together. Devotion to family and future.

title: Money and work in married relationship
description: Times when these women worked out. Earned money belonged to the couple, whoever earned it.

title: Marie Wurman cuts her sister's finger off; quilts
description: Mamie Wurman cut her sister's finger off with a hatchet by mistake. Quilt stitching - why would a woman cut the material up to put it back together?

title: Foreigners and Indians
description: Foreigners from Missouri. How these women reacted to the Indians as children. Arkansans see Indians for the first time.

title: Horse contests and Native American horses
description: Horse pulling contests - a man is knocked unconscious for interfering. A poor looking Indian sorrel cleans up at a horse race.

title: Experiences with gypsies. Lumberjacks.
description: Experiences with gypsies. Lumberjacks.
